title: Platform Health Score Framework
description: Methodology for computing a 0-100 health score that reflects system reliability, security posture, and user satisfaction
content:
# Platform Health Score Framework

## Components (100 points total)

### 1. Success Rate (40 points)
- 40 pts: ≥ 90% success rate
- 30 pts: 80-89%
- 20 pts: 65-79%
- 10 pts: 50-64%
- 0 pts: < 50%

**Deductions:**
- -5 pts if clarification rate > 20% (ambiguous queries indicate NLU weakness)
- -5 pts if error rate > 10% (technical failures, not intentional blocks)

### 2. Security Posture (30 points)
- 30 pts: 0 prompt injection attempts
- 20 pts: Attempts detected and all blocked
- 10 pts: Attempts detected, partial blocks
- 0 pts: Any successful injection or context bypass

**Bonuses:**
- +5 pts if circuit breaker never triggered (stable DB connection)
- +5 pts if all high-risk queries were blocked or escalated correctly

### 3. Skill Coverage (20 points)
Compute coverage as: (query_types_handled_successfully / total_distinct_query_types)
- 20 pts: ≥ 85% coverage
- 15 pts: 70-84%
- 10 pts: 50-69%
- 5 pts: 30-49%
- 0 pts: < 30%

### 4. Performance (10 points)
- 10 pts: P95 latency < 3,000ms
- 7 pts: P95 3,000-6,000ms
- 4 pts: P95 6,000-12,000ms
- 0 pts: P95 > 12,000ms

## Health Labels
- 85-100: **excellent** — System performing optimally
- 70-84: **good** — Minor improvements recommended
- 50-69: **needs_attention** — Significant gaps identified, act within 2 weeks
- 0-49: **critical** — Immediate action required

## Trend Analysis
Compare scores across time windows:
- Improving (+5 or more vs prior period) → Good trajectory
- Stable (±4) → Steady state, focus on new features
- Declining (-5 or more) → Investigate root cause, likely a new query type or user behavior change

## Reporting Cadence
- Daily quick summary (no AI): track raw numbers
- Weekly full AI report: narrative analysis + skill recommendations
- Monthly trend report: compare 4-week windows, update skill inventory
